metadata.name: CLUTRR kinship gold graphs
metadata.description: CLUTRR (Sinha et al., EMNLP 2019) semi-synthetic family stories standardized to per-story gold KINSHIP graphs: entity nodes (surface, gender, mention spans) + typed atomic story facts (the proof chain) + a held-out query edge requiring >=2-hop composition + structural absent-relation pairs (different connected components) + structural noise edges. One end-to-end venue for atomic-extraction P/R, multi-hop accuracy vs chain length, human-auditable trace-graphs, and a hallucination demo on provably-absent relations.
metadata.schema: exp_sel_data_out (input=story string, output=json.dumps(gold_graph), structured per-row data under metadata_* keys).

clutrr_gen.doc: Clean stories. Train hops 2-3-4, test hops 2..10 -> the multi-hop accuracy-vs-chain-length curve + atomic-extraction P/R + trace-graph.

clutrr_disc.doc: Disconnected-fact noise: a second, structurally separate family chain => genuine within-document ABSENT-relation pairs for the hallucination demo.

composition_table.semantics: Triple (h, r, t) reads 't is h's r'. Composition: A t1 B, B t2 C => A t3 C, where rules[t1][t2]=t3 and the surface form of t3 uses the gender of C.
composition_table.note: Finite composition table over kinship relation TYPES (facebookresearch/clutrr rules_store.yaml). NOT a full relation algebra: no general intersection/converse closure beyond these rules; some compositions yield no-relation; ambiguous compositions (e.g. grand o inv-child) are intentionally excluded in CLUTRR. Use to temper any generality claim.

source.scope: family relation type only (CLUTRR 'work' relation type excluded; the delivered configs are all kinship).

gold_graph_field_spec.edges: typed ATOMIC story facts (the proof chain); {source,target,kinship_relation,relation_type,is_query=false,hop_count=1}; directed: t is source's kinship_relation (i.e. (h,r,t) with h=source).
gold_graph_field_spec.noise_edges: extra story_edges CLUTRR does NOT type (disconnected/distractor facts); structural only {source,target,relation_type=null}. The word-before-bracket heuristic recovers only ~54% even on known edges, so noise relations are intentionally left untyped, not fabricated.
gold_graph_field_spec.query_edge: held-out, NOT stated; deduced by composing the atomic edges; {source,target,kinship_relation=gold,relation_type,target_int,hop_count}.
gold_graph_field_spec.absent_relation_pairs: entity pairs in DIFFERENT connected components of the story graph => provably no kinship path => 'no-relation' (structural, conservative, sound). Capped at 20/story.

reconstruction_facts.node_id_to_name: genders is canonically ordered by node id; verified consistent with the backward-chaining proof leaves on all rows.
reconstruction_facts.atomic_edges: = story_edges[:len(edge_types)] zipped with edge_types (the proof chain is always the prefix; verified 0 violations).
reconstruction_facts.leaves: proof_state sub-triples that are never a goal key (= the atomic facts).
reconstruction_facts.directionality: story_edge (s,t) with edge_types[k]=r encodes leaf (name[s], r, name[t]) = 'name[t] is name[s]'s r'.

provenance.dataset: CLUTRR: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Inductive Reasoning from Text
provenance.csv_source: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kliang5/CLUTRR_huggingface_dataset/main (the GitHub raw mirror HF CLUTRR/v1 v1.py downloads)
provenance.license: CLUTRR is released by Facebook under a research/non-commercial license (see facebookresearch/clutrr LICENSE; CC-BY-NC-style). Use for research only.

metadata.boundary_note: DESCRIPTIVE counts only. No composition/closure, all-pair labeling, atomic-extraction P/R, multi-hop accuracy, N*, or hallucination-rate numbers, and no LLM calls -- those are iter-3's experiment.

examples.input: Clarence wanted to visit his son, Wayne for the holidays. He also wanted to see Wayne's daughter Heidi.
examples.metadata_clean_story: [Clarence] wanted to visit his son, [Wayne] for the holidays. He also wanted to see [Wayne]'s daughter [Heidi].

examples.input: Lena invited her brother Joshua, his wife Lynn and their daughter Andrea over for dinner last Sunday.
examples.metadata_clean_story: [Lena] invited her brother [Joshua], his wife [Lynn] and their daughter [Andrea] over for dinner last Sunday.

examples.input: Harold bought a book for his son Robert. Harold decided to take his son, Antonio, on a ski vacation. Antonio called his brother Louis on Friday because it was his birthday. Louis was proud of his son, Wayne.
examples.metadata_clean_story: [Harold] bought a book for his son [Robert]. [Harold] decided to take his son, [Antonio], on a ski vacation. [Antonio] called his brother [Louis] on Friday because it was his birthday. [Louis] was proud of his son, [Wayne].

examples.input: Aurora's nephew, Leo, recently made the high school swim team. Cruz is taking his son Robert on a camping trip for the weekend. Robert and his daughter Clara like to read poems together.

examples.metadata_clean_story: [Robert] and his daughter [Clara] like to read poems together. [Cruz] is taking his son [Robert] on a camping trip for the weekend.

examples.input: Johnny called his brother, Justin, to confirm when Justin's sister, Roberta, was coming to the dinner. David and his grandmother Maida went to the ice cream shop/ Johnny's grandfather, Stephen, asked the boy what he wanted for Christmas. Johnny told him he wanted a remote controlled car.
